boundary.note: Claim_atom says 'covers Pg adapter+project+workspace+sync' but content only describes Drizzle query execution (select/insert/update/delete), transactions, afterCommit hooks. Project, workspace, and sync are not mentioned anywhere in the change description. This is a claim/content mismatch. Additionally, steps 1 (package.json glob) and 2 (CI test.yml glob) are already implemented by PG-002 uncommitted work — the plan is partially redundant without acknowledging it.

coupling.note: Acknowledges PG-002 in 'Current State' section but does not declare it as a hard dependency (PG-003 requires PG-002 to be merged first). Since PG-002 is still status:proposed/revision 5, the glob changes in PG-003 depend on the PG-002 CI job existing. If PG-002 is reverted or its CI job structure changes, PG-003's changes break. This should be stated explicitly.

safety.note: Two safety issues: (1) DuckDB contamination — test/storage/adapter.pg.test.ts contains both PgAdapter tests (lines 69-270) AND DuckDBAdapter tests (lines 273-457). The glob test/**/*.pg.test.ts will pick up the DuckDB tests in the CI pg job. DuckDB may not be available or may fail in the pg CI environment. (2) The proposed new test file 'pg-integration.test.ts' does NOT match the glob pattern '*.pg.test.ts', so it won't be auto-discovered by the test:pg script or CI pg job.

reversibility.note: Changes touch 2 existing files (package.json, test.yml) and 1 new file. If step 3 is the only remaining work, reverting the new test file is trivially clean. The glob changes in steps 1-2 are already applied (part of PG-002 uncommitted work), so their reversibility is tied to PG-002. Within PG-003's scope: cleanly reversible.

surface_area.note: 3 files changed/created is minimal. However, steps 1-2 are already done (PG-002 pre-applied), so the actual remaining work is just creating pg-integration.test.ts. The plan should either (a) be updated to reflect that steps 1-2 are complete, or (b) explicitly verify the glob changes are not already applied before re-committing.

convention.note: The *.pg.test.ts naming convention is established by PG-002 (adapter.pg.test.ts, migration.pg.test.ts). The proposed new test file name 'pg-integration.test.ts' violates this convention — it should be 'pg-integration.pg.test.ts' to be auto-discovered by the glob. The file name must end with '*.pg.test.ts' for the glob to match it.

coverage.note: The plan aims to ensure all Pg tests pass against both PGlite and real Postgres, but: (1) The new integration test won't be included in the CI run due to naming mismatch. (2) DuckDB tests in adapter.pg.test.ts will run in the pg CI job and may fail. (3) The 'verify tests pass' step is undefined — no specific test command, no pass/fail criteria, no expected results. (4) No discussion of how pgTestLayer from test/fixture/pg.ts is used by the new integration test.

objections.summary: DuckDBAdapter tests in adapter.pg.test.ts will be picked up by test/**/*.pg.test.ts glob and run in the CI pg job
objections.detail: The DuckDB test section (describe('DuckDBAdapter')) creates ManagedRuntime.make(DatabaseAdapter.DuckDBAdapter) which spawns a DuckDB subprocess. In the pg CI job environment, DuckDB binary may not be available or may fail. These tests are conceptually unrelated to the 'pg' CI job.

objections.summary: New test file 'pg-integration.test.ts' does not match '*.pg.test.ts' naming convention
objections.detail: The test:pg script and CI pg job both use glob test/**/*.pg.test.ts. The proposed filename 'test/storage/pg-integration.test.ts' ends with .test.ts, not .pg.test.ts. This test would never be executed by the pg CI job, defeating its purpose. Should be renamed to 'pg-integration.pg.test.ts'.

objections.summary: Claim says 'covers Pg adapter+project+workspace+sync' but content only describes Drizzle query execution
objections.detail: The claim_atom list includes project, workspace, and sync coverage which are not present in the change description. The content only describes Drizzle query execution (select/insert/update/delete), transactions, and afterCommit hooks. This is a significant scope mismatch.

objections.summary: New integration test doesn't specify integration with pgTestLayer or schema isolation approach
objections.detail: The plan mentions 'Schema isolation (via pgTestLayer)' but doesn't specify whether the test uses the convenience pgTestLayer from test/fixture/pg.ts, or builds its own layer. Since pgTestLayer handles both PGlite and real Postgres switching, the test should explicitly use pgTestLayer to inherit CI/local dual-mode support.

objections.summary: Step 4 'Verify tests pass' has no specific test command or pass criteria
objections.detail: The plan says 'Run bun test test/**/*.pg.test.ts locally, fix any failures' but doesn't specify: (a) which working directory, (b) the exact command (should be 'bun run test:pg' not 'bun test ...'), (c) what constitutes passing (all tests? specific subset?), (d) that the test must pass with both PGlite and (when available) real Postgres.

conditions_for_approval: Rename the proposed test file from 'pg-integration.test.ts' to 'pg-integration.pg.test.ts' to match the glob convention
Add a note in the plan or in the test file acknowledging that adapter.pg.test.ts contains DuckDB tests which will run in the pg CI job, with a justification or plan to split them
Align claim_atoms with actual content — remove 'project+workspace+sync' from claim_atom 3 if those are not tested
Make step 1 and 2 conditional on whether the glob is already present (PG-002 state), or acknowledge the pre-applied state
Add explicit test command and pass criteria to step 4 (e.g., 'Run `bun run test:pg` from packages/opencode, expect 0 failures')

follow_up_issues.summary: Split DuckDB tests out of adapter.pg.test.ts into a dedicated file (e.g., adapter.duckdb.test.ts) so the pg CI job doesn't run DuckDB tests
follow_up_issues.rationale: The file name implies Pg-only tests but contains both PgAdapter and DuckDBAdapter tests. The DuckDB tests should live separately so the *.pg.test.ts glob accurately represents Postgres-only test files.

follow_up_issues.summary: Add test for pgTestLayer's Postgres schema isolation cleanup — verify that real Postgres schema is dropped after test suite completes
follow_up_issues.rationale: makeRealPgLayer creates an isolated schema per test invocation but has no teardown. If tests are interrupted, schemas accumulate. This is especially important for CI runs.
